parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf10411-candidate`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Normalize Candidate Payload

### Normalize Candidate Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming candidate records to a stable schema

Next: Check Calendar Availability

### Check Calendar Availability

googleCalendar — `getAll` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Slot Free? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Slot Free?

Two-way gate on `={{ $items().length === 0 || !$json.id }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Create Calendar Event · ②→ Propose Alternative Slots

### Create Calendar Event

googleCalendar — `create` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Confirmation (Gmail) · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Send Confirmation (Gmail)

gmail — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Hold Until Reminder Time · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Propose Alternative Slots

gmail — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Hold Until Reminder Time

Pauses 1 hours — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Send Reminder (Gmail)

### Send Reminder (Gmail)

gmail — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Sort Candidates Newest First · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Sort Candidates Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Send Summary to Gmail

### Send Summary to Gmail

gmail — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Onboard new customers with Trello and Gmail automatically

**Pattern:** Onboarding Sequence · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Onboard new customers with Trello and Gmail automatically. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual surveys is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Normalize Candidate Payload → Check Calendar Availability → Slot Free? → Create Calendar Event → Send Confirmation (Gmail) → Hold Until Reminder Time → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to onboarding sequence without manual effort
- Anyone automating surveys in human resources
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Scales without extra headcount
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your surveys process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
